reasoning: The vulnerable code path (_httpencode returning an unescaped value into Content-Disposition) exists in output_to_destination() for dest='I' and dest='D', but there is exactly one call site in the entire plugin: class-woe-formatter-pdf.php line 489 calls `$this->pdf->output_to_destination('f', $this->filename)` — dest='f' (case 'F': file save). The 'I' (inline browser) and 'D' (download) branches that emit HTTP headers are dead code in this plugin. No AJAX handler, REST endpoint, or any other code invokes output_to_destination with a browser-output destination. The theoretical sink (_httpencode with no CRLF stripping) is real, but the reachability claim is false: no attacker-controlled request can reach the header-emitting branches.
